summary: Backend: 25/25 pytest tests PASSED covering health/meta, auth (register/login/me/lockout/admin), generation flow (demo mode + credit deduction + 402), prompt enhance (Claude via Emergent LLM), feed/like/remix, battle/vote (incl. double-vote 400), leaderboard, challenges, pricing & Stripe checkout. Frontend: BLOCKED — Landing page crashes on mount with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading ReactCurrentOwner)' thrown from @react-three/fiber. Root cause: React 19.0.0 used with @react-three/fiber@8.x — fiber v8 references React internals (ReactCurrentOwner) that no longer exist in React 19. The dev-overlay error blocks the whole UI so no further UI flow could be tested.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout key is f'{request.client.host}:{email}'. Behind K8s ingress requests rotate between multiple upstream IPs (e.g. 10.232.129.197 and 10.232.133.130 observed in backend logs), so 5 wrong attempts get split into 2-3 buckets and the lockout often does NOT trigger from a single external client. Test only saw 429 after ~12 attempts (vs spec'd 5). Use email-only or X-Forwarded-For from trusted proxy as the bucket key.

Does not exclude or convert MongoDB `_id`. The _hydrate helper renames `_id` -> `id`, so this is fine for the list — verified — but newly-created records via /api/generate/text-to-3d return a synthetic `{id, status}` without the rest of the document. UI must rely on polling /api/generate/{id} (which it does), so no functional issue.

ui_bugs.component: Landing (and any page that mounts ModelViewer / Canvas)
ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'ReactCurrentOwner') thrown from @react-three/fiber 8.x reconciler. React 19 removed the internal __SECRET_INTERNALS_DO_NOT_USE.ReactCurrentOwner symbol that R3F 8 depends on. The entire landing page is replaced by the red 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay; no UI elements (hero-cta-primary, nav, etc.) become interactable. ALL downstream UI tests (register, studio, feed, battle, challenges, pricing) were blocked by this.

action_items: FRONTEND CRITICAL: Upgrade to @react-three/fiber@^9 (React 19 compatible) AND @react-three/drei@^10 (which pairs with R3F v9). Alternatively pin react/react-dom to ^18.3 if you must keep R3F 8/drei 9.x. After yarn install, restart frontend and reload the landing page — the ReactCurrentOwner error should be gone. Without this fix the whole UI is unusable.

BACKEND (security, low-medium): change brute-force lockout bucket from '{client_ip}:{email}' to just email (or read X-Forwarded-For). Behind multi-replica ingress the current scheme is partially bypassable.
Re-run the full Playwright UI suite once R3F is upgraded so that register→studio→generate→feed→battle→pricing flows can be validated end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_utils.get_current_user imports `from server import db` inside the function — works but creates a circular-import smell. Consider moving db to a separate db.py module.

generation_routes._run_mock_generation runs as a BackgroundTask but updates the DB directly without try/except — a Mongo hiccup would leave the generation stuck in 'pending' forever and no retry. Add error handling and a TTL/cleanup job for stale pending docs.
community_routes.random_battle inserts a NEW battle doc on every GET — bots could fill the `battles` collection unbounded. Consider rate-limiting or a TTL index on `created_at` for open battles.
models.py PromptEnhanceRequest.prompt max_length=200 while GenerationRequest.prompt is 500 — inconsistent and the studio UI doesn't surface this limit (will 422 silently).

payment_routes derives `host_url` from `request.base_url` (internal pod URL like http://0.0.0.0:8001/) for the Stripe webhook_url — webhooks from Stripe will never reach this address. Use a public URL from env (e.g. PUBLIC_BASE_URL).
CORS is open ('*') with allow_credentials=False; fine for now, but tighten before production.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@bloxcraft.ai / BloxCraft2026!. New users registered freely via /api/auth/register; each starts with 20 credits.
seed_data_creation: Backend lifespan seeds admin user, 7 challenges, and 6 demo generations on first start. Pytest creates fresh users per session with @example.com (the .test TLD is rejected by pydantic EmailStr).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid (25/25 passing). The ONLY blocker is @react-three/fiber 8 vs React 19. Once main agent bumps fiber/drei (or downgrades React), re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py for regression and re-run the Playwright flow that was prepared in this iteration (register → studio generate → feed sort/like → battle vote → pricing checkout). Note for Playwright: use @example.com for register emails (pydantic EmailStr rejects @bloxcraft.test).
rca_of_the_issue: Landing.jsx (and Studio.jsx) render the <ModelViewer/> component which mounts a <Canvas> from @react-three/fiber@8.18. fiber 8's reconciler reads `React.__CLIENT_INTERNALS_DO_NOT_USE_OR_WARN_USERS_THEY_CANNOT_UPGRADE.ReactCurrentOwner` (or the older `__SECRET_INTERNALS_…`) to wire up its custom renderer. React 19 removed this symbol — accessing it returns undefined, so `ReactCurrentOwner.current` throws. Fix: yarn add @react-three/fiber@^9 @react-three/drei@^10 (R3F v9 was released specifically for React 19 support). After upgrade, no code changes are required in ModelViewer.jsx (the public API is backwards compatible).
